component.name: ErrorState
component.description: Centered error or empty-page composition with an icon badge, title, description, optional error detail block, and action slot. Pairs with EmptyState as the negative-path counterpart.

commonPatterns.name: runtime-error
commonPatterns.description: Destructive tone for system or runtime errors with a Try Again action
commonPatterns.composition: <ErrorState icon={CircleAlert} title="Something went wrong" description="We couldn't load that resource. Please try again."><Button onClick={retry}>Try again</Button></ErrorState>

commonPatterns.name: not-found
error-with-detail
commonPatterns.description: Surface the underlying error message or stack trace via errorDetail
commonPatterns.composition: <ErrorState icon={CircleAlert} title="Build failed" description="The deploy did not finish." errorDetail={<CodeSnippet>{stackTrace}</CodeSnippet>}><Button onClick={retry}>Retry</Button></ErrorState>

commonPatterns.name: string-error-detail
commonPatterns.description: Inline monospace error block when no copy-to-clipboard is needed
commonPatterns.composition: <ErrorState icon={CircleAlert} title="Validation failed" description="Please fix the highlighted fields." errorDetail="required: ['name','email']" />

antiPatterns.scenario: Using ErrorState for empty data states
antiPatterns.reason: ErrorState communicates a problem (failure, missing, permission); empty data is a neutral, expected state
antiPatterns.alternative: Use EmptyState for empty lists, search results, or first-run scenarios

antiPatterns.scenario: Using destructive tone for a 404 page
antiPatterns.reason: 404s are not errors per se; destructive coloring overstates severity
antiPatterns.alternative: Use tone='neutral' for not-found and similar non-failure states

antiPatterns.scenario: Stuffing the errorDetail with copy that belongs in description
antiPatterns.reason: errorDetail is for technical/diagnostic info (stack traces, error codes); description is for the human-readable explanation
antiPatterns.alternative: Keep description user-friendly; reserve errorDetail for technical strings or CodeSnippet content

semanticMeaning.destructive: Runtime errors, system failures — destructive ring around icon badge, destructive-foreground icon
semanticMeaning.neutral: 404s, not-found, permission-denied — muted icon and neutral ring with elevation

slots.icon: Icon prop accepts any LucideIcon component; rendered inside a 48×48 badge at the top center
slots.errorDetail: Optional ReactNode (e.g. CodeSnippet) or string (rendered as monospace block) shown between description and actions
slots.actions: children slot at the bottom, intended for one or more Button components, centered with gap-4

parentConstraints: Should typically be the centerpiece of the page — give it room to breathe (do not nest in cramped containers)

interactions.static: ErrorState is a display composition; interactivity comes from the children action buttons

responsive.mobile: Centers and constrains to max-w-[620px]; padding remains p-14
responsive.desktop: Same layout; max-w-[620px] keeps the composition readable on wide screens

accessibility.role: Composition wraps a heading (h2) and supporting paragraph; icon is decorative
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Action buttons in children slot are individually focusable
accessibility.screenReader: Reads as: title (h2) → description → optional error detail → action labels
accessibility.focusManagement: First focus goes to the first action button when present

rules: Always supply icon, title, and description — they are required props for clarity
Choose tone based on intent: destructive for failures, neutral for not-found / permission states
Use errorDetail for technical info (stack traces, error codes); keep description human-readable
Pass action buttons as children — typically a Button to retry or navigate home
Pair with EmptyState for the empty-data counterpart; do not overload ErrorState for empty states

aiHints.context: Use for full-page or full-section error displays — not inline alerts. Pick tone='destructive' for runtime/system errors and tone='neutral' for 404s and permission-denied. Pass an action Button as children so users have a recovery path. Use EmptyState (not ErrorState) for empty data states.
